Widnes were the defending [[2010 National League Cup]] champions after they won the competition in 2009. ==Off Field Activity== ===2010 Strip=== The Widnes Vikings unveiled their new 2010 home shirt on Tuesday 1 December 2009 in front of 300 fans at the [[Halton Stadium|Stobart Stadium Halton]].{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/vikings/vikingsnews/4773852.Vikings_unveil_new_kit_and_squad_numbers/ |title=Vikings unveil new kit and squad numbers |accessdate=2009-12-02 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes World |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722215511/http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/vikings/vikingsnews/4773852.Vikings_unveil_new_kit_and_squad_numbers/ |archivedate=2011-07-22 |url-status=live }} Manufactured by Irish-based [[O'Neills|O'Neill's Sportswear]], the shirt was specially designed with advances moisture control allowing the body to breathe easily with the garment feeling dry and cool to contribute to improved player performance. The main shirt sponsor was the [[Stobart Group]], and the word 'Stobart' was written across the front of the shirt. The strip also had four coloured lines (1 black, 2 red and another black line) stretching up the left hand side of the shirt; these four lines were similar to the three red lines the Stobart Group use on their [[Logistics|haulage]] vehicles. [[File:Eddie Stobart lorry Scania R420 Sandra Ann PX07 BXK in Plymouth, 9 August 2007.jpg|thumb|right|175px|''Eddie Stobart lorry. The Stobart Group were the main sponsors for the Vikingss in 2010''.|alt=Eddie Stobart lorry. The Stobart Group are the main sponsors for the Vikingss in 2010.]] The shirt also featured the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]]'s 'Try Assist' logo, the new name for the RFL Benevolent Fund and a new sleeve sponsor for 2010 in Halton Housing Trust. The logo of [[Borough of Halton|Halton Borough Council]] was also displayed on the right hand side of the chest on the front of the shirt. During the event the 2010 squad numbers were announced and fans were able to pre-order the new shirt which the Vikings will wear against [[Warrington Wolves]] in the World of Beds Cup on [[Boxing Day]]. For the Gateshead Thunder game in 2009, chairman Steve O'Connor offered free admission for all Vikings fans. As season ticket holders did not benefit from this special offer (as they had already paid for the game when purchasing their season ticket), the club decided they would give vouchers to the 2009 season ticket holders who renewed their season tickets during an 'Early Bird' promotion. The vouchers were for fans to use on kit manufacturer O'Neills merchandise in the January sales.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/10/widnes-vikings-voucher-offer-for-loyal-vikings-fans-55368-25356262/ |title=Voucher offer for loyal Vikings fans |accessdate=2009-12-10 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022901/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/10/widnes-vikings-voucher-offer-for-loyal-vikings-fans-55368-25356262/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} Fans could claim discounts on Vikings items from the store, including the new 2010 strip. The discounts were: adult season ticket holders £10, concession season ticket holders £7 and junior season ticket holders £2. These discounts could only be applied when visiting the O'Neils store in [[Widnes]] town centre. Vikings' CEO Alex Bonney explained the thinking behind the initiative: "Steve's offer in June came when things weren't going well on the pitch and the comeback against Gateshead, plus the atmosphere generated by our fans, set the team up for the victory over Halifax in the NRC semi-final and ultimately the final victory over Barrow to give us the ability to apply for a 2012 Super League licence. "While recognising that season ticket holders had already paid for this game as part of their 2009 ticket, we had planned to discount their 2010 season ticket but were heartened that a number of fans spoke to us to say that they understood the 'bigger picture' and were keen to back the club's initiative for that game without recompense. "With that in mind, and in conjunction with O'Neill's, the voucher scheme was established. "However, for those fans who do not wish to avail of this offer, we will class their voucher as a donation to the Valhalla Foundation, our award-winning charity."" ====Special Edition Charity Kit==== [[File:Widnes-vikings-2010-charity-kit.jpg|thumb|left|175px|''Paddy Flynn & Chris Gerrard modelling the Vikings special edition charity kit''.|alt=Paddy Flynn & Chris Gerrard modelling the Vikings special edition charity kit.]]On Thursday 18 March Widnes wore a specially designed "one-off" charity kit during their Co-Operative Championship match, that was broadcast live on [[Sky Sports]] against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] to promote the [[Everyman Campaign]], which raises awareness of [[testicle|testicular]] and [[prostate]] [[cancer]] in men.{{cite web | url=http://everyman-campaign.org/News/Press_Archive/2010/14960.shtml | title=Prostatakrebs - eine Everyman-Campaign Aufklärung mit Studien, Statistiken, Behandlungsmethoden und alternativen Therapien | access-date=24 March 2010 | archive-date=4 April 2010 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100404041822/http://www.everyman-campaign.org/News/Press_Archive/2010/14960.shtml | url-status=dead }} The specially designed kit was manufactured by the Vikings current kit manufacturer O'Neils. The design of the kit was featured around a "[[nude]]" look, with the shirt made to look like a bare chested male [[torso]], and the shorts made to look like males [[thighs]], with a [[fig leaf]] covering the [[genital]] area.{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id%3D1065 |title = Official Widnes Vikings RLFC Web Site |accessdate=2010-03-24 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120223002247/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=1065 |archivedate=2012-02-23 }} Fans could purchase the special edition shirt from Wednesday 10 March. The shirt cost £40 and in aid of event O'Neil's donated £5 from every shirt sale to the Everyman charity. Fans were also able to make a donation online through a special page set up by the Vikings on the [[Justgiving]] website. Fans were also able to donate on the night at the [[Halton Stadium|Stobart Stadium Halton]] as volunteers were available within the stadium to collect donations. To raise further funds for the charity, fans were able to purchase the match shirts worn by the players on the night through an [[auction]] run by the club. Fans were given until 5.00 pm on Monday 22 March to submit a bid to the club. Bids opened up at a minimum price of £50.00 and bids could be submitted by e-mail or by leaving a bid in a sealed envelope at the club reception.{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id%3D1231 |title = Official Widnes Vikings RLFC Web Site |accessdate=2010-03-24 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120223002252/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=1231 |archivedate=2012-02-23 }} Vikings chairman Steve O'Connor said: "Nearly everyone knows someone who has been affected by cancer. Unfortunately among men it is still a problem we don't like to talk about. We want to help raise awareness of the symptoms - and how to get help - and also help to raise money for such an important cause. We have chosen the Halifax game because we are expecting a big crowd so we can make a significant donation to Everyman". Everyman spokesperson Tatjana Trposka said: "We are thrilled with the support we have received from Widnes Vikings. We desperately need to raise more awareness and funds to fight prostate and testicular cancers and we are another step closer thanks to the help of Widnes." ===Transfers & Squad=== It was all change for Widnes during pre-season with eight popular first team players leaving the Stobart Stadium Halton for pastures new. [[Gavin Dodd]], [[John Duffy (rugby league)|John Duffy]], [[Richard Fletcher (rugby league)|Richard Fletcher]], [[Tim Hartley]], Michael Ostick and [[Brett Robinson (rugby league)|Brett Robinson]] all left in transfer deals, whilst [[Danny Mills (rugby league)|Danny Mills]] and [[Lee Paterson]] were released. Many of the moves were forced due to [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] reducing the salary cap.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/08/13/widnes-vikings-co-operative-championship-salary-cap-to-be-lowered-for-2010-55368-24383004/ |title=Co-operative Championship salary cap to be lowered for 2010 |accessdate=2009-08-13 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022917/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/08/13/widnes-vikings-co-operative-championship-salary-cap-to-be-lowered-for-2010-55368-24383004/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} Previously the Championship's salary cap operated by limiting a club's player costs to no more than 50% of the club's income and is capped at a limit of £400,000. The new figures for 2010 will be reduced to 40% and £300,000, respectively. Sporting Director [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league)|Terry O'Connor]] said: "I know some of the players leaving would prefer to stay and we would like to keep them but with the reduction of the salary cap to £300,000, we have to spend accordingly".{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/10/01/widnes-vikings-star-names-depart-stobart-stadium-halton-55368-24820168/ |title=Star names depart Stobart Stadium Halton |accessdate=2009-10-01 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022837/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/10/01/widnes-vikings-star-names-depart-stobart-stadium-halton-55368-24820168/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} ====Transfers in==== {| class="wikitable" width="65%" style="font-size:95%" |- ! colspan=4 | Players In |- ! The dual registration system is different to a loan agreement. Dual registered players can be called back at any time by their original club, when needed to play for them intermittently based upon form or injuries. When they are not needed by their original club they are eligible to play for their alternative dual registered club. A player is only eligible for dual registration if they are outside the top 20 players (financially) of the Super League club's playing squad. Dual registered players are not allowed to feature in [[Challenge Cup]] games for their alternative club. Loan players may go to Championship Clubs or to Super League clubs but must go for a minimum of 4 weeks. {| class="wikitable" width="65%" style="font-size:95%" |- ! colspan=5 | Loan/Dual Registration |- ! Paul (Cullen) and Terry (O'Connor) have recruited well. | source = [[Stuart Wilkinson (rugby league)|Stuart Wilkinson]] | width =250px | align =left }}The international rugby league season took place between the 2009 and 2010 seasons, with the [[2009 Four Nations|Four Nations]] being held in [[England]] and [[France]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/Four_Nations_2009/venues.html |title=Four Nations 2009 Venues |accessdate=25 August 2016 |publisher=Rugby League Project |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121009080016/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/Four_Nations_2009/venues.html |archivedate=9 October 2012 |url-status=live }} Vikings assistant coach [[Stuart Wilkinson (rugby league)|Stuart Wilkinson]] assisted former Widnes star [[Bobbie Goulding]] by leading the [[France national rugby league team|French]] national team into the Four Nations,{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/11/19/widnes-vikings-assistant-coach-stuart-wilkinson-brings-fresh-ideas-back-to-stobart-stadium-halton-from-france-after-four-nations-exploits-55368-25198874/ |title=Widnes Vikings: Assistant coach Stuart Wilkinson brings fresh ideas back to Stobart Stadium Halton from France after Four Nations exploits |accessdate=2009-11-19 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022530/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/11/19/widnes-vikings-assistant-coach-stuart-wilkinson-brings-fresh-ideas-back-to-stobart-stadium-halton-from-france-after-four-nations-exploits-55368-25198874/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} against [[England national rugby league team|England]], [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. France's opening game was against England in the [[Keepmoat Stadium]] in [[Doncaster]].{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8318747.stm |title=England 34-12 France|access-date=2009-10-23|publisher=BBC Sport}} Wilkinson and Goulding's French team started off strong taking a 12–4 lead at half time. England put in a strong second half performance scoring 30 unanswered point to win the game 34–12. Results didn't get much better for the French side as they were punished 62-12{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8334785.stm|title=France 12-62 New Zealand|access-date=2009-10-31|publisher=BBC Sport | date=2009-10-31}} by New Zealand in their second game of the competition and defeated 42-4{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8339883.stm|title=France 4-42 Australia|access-date=2009-11-07|publisher=BBC Sport | date=2009-11-07}} by Australia in their final game. Despite losing all three games Wilkinson believes the experience will prove invaluable to him as a coach as he gained valuable insights on some new training and tactical ideas he plans to introduce to the training pitch at Widnes. He said: "It's great working with and against the game's elite and pitting your wits against the best players and coaches in the world. It will be the first time former Wolves player [[Steve Pickersgill]] faces up against his old club after his transfer to the Vikings during the off season. Vikings Head Coach [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] also a former Warrington Wolves player stated that the tie is not about "me versus Warrington", it's about getting a good run-out for some of our players and raising some funds.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/24/widnes-vikings-paul-cullen-to-experiment-with-new-squad-against-old-club-warrington-wolves-55368-25449397/ |title=Paul Cullen to experiment with new squad against old club Warrington Wolves |accessdate=2009-12-24 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022611/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/24/widnes-vikings-paul-cullen-to-experiment-with-new-squad-against-old-club-warrington-wolves-55368-25449397/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} However, [[Winter of 2009–2010 in the United Kingdom|freezing weather conditions]] led to the game being cancelled as the surface was unplayable, and road conditions treacherous for fans. |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} by getting the Widnes side ready for a seaside clash with [[Blackpool Panthers]] in the absence of head coach [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] who was granted compassionate leave by the club whilst his wife is receiving treatment for [[cancer]].{{cite web |url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=593 |title=Paul Cullen granted compassionate leave |accessdate=2010-01-03 |publisher=Widnes Vikings |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100121110211/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=593 |archivedate=2010-01-21 |url-status=dead }} The snow and ice has meant the temporary duo of Wilkinson and Cassidy have had the unenviable task of finding alternative methods of preparing the squad for the Blackpool fixture. Wilkinson is happy to do all he can to help out during a difficult time for the club, he said: "I love the coaching work that I do and I have no aspiration to be a head coach, but it's a case of needs must at the moment. We're working in an unusual circumstances and Paul will be in and out. We're all supporting Paul in the best way we can and so myself and Mick Cassidy will ensure everything runs like he wants it to." Despite all the effort put in on both sides to prepare for the fixture, the [[Winter of 2009–2010 in the United Kingdom|freezing weather conditions]] have again interrupted the Viking's pre season schedule. Due to snow and ice the pitch has been deemed unplayable and the Blackpool Panthers vs Widnes Vikings game was cancelled.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2010/01/08/vikings-to-contest-karalius-cup-after-blackpool-game-cancelled-55368-25557610/ |title=Vikings to Helens in the 2010 Karalius Cup.]]2,034 fans braved the bitterly cold weather as the [[Widnes Vikings]] suffered a 42–26 defeat at the hands of [[Super League]] giants [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the Karalius Cup. This is the second time the sides have met in the Karalius Cup which was set up in honour of [[Vince Karalius]] who was a well decorated player for both Widnes and St Helens in the 1950s, and 1960s. Vince died from cancer in 2008.{{cite news|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/comment/obituaries/article5354363.ece |title=Vince Karalius: rugby league player and manager |access-date=2008-12-17 |publisher=Times Online |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100524074529/http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/comment/obituaries/article5354363.ece |archive-date=2010-05-24 |location=London |date=2008-12-17 |url-status=dead }} This was the first run out the players had had in the 2010 campaign after the Vikings pre-season preparations were hampered by severe weather conditions, dubbed the [[Winter of 2009–2010 in the United Kingdom|Big Freeze]] by the media. Severe snowfall had resulted in unplayable conditions with caused both the [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Blackpool Panthers]] games to be cancelled.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2010/01/04/widnes-vikings-warrington-wolves-game-cancelled-55368-25520978/ |title=Widnes Vikings: Warrington Wolves game cancelled |accessdate=2010-01-04 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022404/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2010/01/04/widnes-vikings-warrington-wolves-game-cancelled-55368-25520978/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} For many of the Vikings players this was the first time they had played in months and was a chance to try to cement their place in the starting line up for the upcoming [[2010 RFL Championship|Co-operative Championship]] season. Saint's arrived at the Stobart Stadium with plenty of players who are expecting to see first team action in this year's [[Super League XV|Super League]] campaign, with Saints imposing an early 22 point lead after four unanswered tries from [[Gary Wheeler (rugby league)|Gary Wheeler]], [[Jamie Foster (rugby league)|Jamie Foster]], [[Iosia Soliola|Sia Soliola]] and [[Bryn Hargreaves]] and 3 conversions from [[Jamie Foster (rugby league)|Jamie Foster]].{{cite web|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/saints/news/4859968.Lomax_shines_as_Saints_cruise_to_Karalius_Cup_win/ |title=Lomax shines as Saints cruise to Karalius Cup win |accessdate=2010-01-19 |publisher=St Helens Start |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130062809/http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/saints/news/4859968.Lomax_shines_as_Saints_cruise_to_Karalius_Cup_win/ |archivedate=2016-01-30 |url-status=live }} Widnes who chose to wear their new 2010 away strip for the clash soon broke through the St Helens defence with two tries{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/vikings/vikingsnews/4859970.Widnes_Vikings_26_St_Helens_42/ |title=Widnes Vikings 26 St Helens 42 |accessdate=2010-01-19 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722215313/http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/vikings/vikingsnews/4859970.Widnes_Vikings_26_St_Helens_42/ |archivedate=2011-07-22 |url-status=live }} from Dean Thompson who was thrust into the starting line up after [[Toa Kohe-Love]] pulled a ham string during the pre-game warm up.{{cite web |url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=684/ |title=Saints defend Karalius Cup in Stobart thriller |accessdate=2010-01-19 |publisher=Widnes Vikings |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128102134/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=684 |archivedate=28 January 2010 |url-status=dead |df=dmy-all }} Shane Grady followed up with successful conversions to add the extras to make it 22–12. [[File:Widnes Vikings vs St Helens Karalius Cup 2010 002.JPG|thumb|right|St Helens getting ready to play the ball during their clash with Widnes in the 2010 Karalius Cup.|alt=St Helens getting ready to play the ball during their clash with Widnes in the 2010 Karalius Cup.]] [[Tom Armstrong (rugby league)|Tom Armstrong]] increased the St Helens lead before a superb 80 metre inter-passing run between [[Anthony Thackeray]] and Danny Hulme with Thackeray going in for the points to score the final try of the half. The Vikings were first off the mark in the second half with a try from Matt Strong who went in over the line from close range as he made the most of his opportunity following a fumble on possession from the Saints. Saints followed up with tries from [[Gary Wheeler (rugby league)|Gary Wheeler]] and dual registered{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/03/widnes-vikings-cullen-excited-by-new-additions-55368-25306287/ |title=Widnes Vikings: Cullen excited by new additions |accessdate=2009-12-03 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022440/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/12/03/widnes-vikings-cullen-excited-by-new-additions-55368-25306287/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} [[Chris Dean (rugby league)|Chris Dean]]. The Saints lead was quickly closed as [[Matt Gardner]] went in for another Widnes try, but this was too little too late as the Saints held out a 10-point lead before [[Jamie Ellis]] finished off the Vikings with another St Helens try. After the game Saints Head coach [[Michael Potter|Mick Potter]] was full of admiration for the Vikings. He said: "I think Widnes have got some real players in there who can really make a impact on the Championship next season. From what I seen out there James Webster could still play Super League and with Mark Smith leading them around the park they have a leader. The forwards are pretty solid too, so I think Widnes look in decent shape ahead of the season." Vikings Head coach [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] was also impressed with the Vikings performance. He said: "I was very happy with what I saw out there. The main thing was the players got some quality game time and got out on the pitch. The likes of Dean Thompson has come in for Toa Kohe-Love on the last minute and he has gone over for two tries and he took them well. Shane Grady has also done well." ====Round 4: vs. Carcassonne==== {{Rugbyleaguebox collapsible |round = Round 4 |date = 23 January 2010 |time = 15:00 GMT |team1 = {{league icon|Carcassonne|15}} Carcassonne |team2 = {{league icon|Widnes Vikings|15}} Widnes Vikings |score = 32–14 |stadium = [[Stade d'Albert Domec]] |location = [[Carcassonne]], [[France]] |attendance = 800 |referee = |points1 = |points2 = |competition = Trial |manofmatch = |report = |1sthalf = 10–10 |2ndhalf = 22–4 }} To celebrate the 70th anniversary of the French club, [[AS Carcassonne|Carcassonne]] and Widnes organised this pre-season friendly in [[France]].{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id%3D649 |title = Official Widnes Vikings RLFC Web Site |accessdate=2010-01-24 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111001002329/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=649 |archivedate=2011-10-01 }} The match against Carcassonne, the club with best historical record in France, was well attended with more than 800{{cite web |url=http://www.com1site.com/FFRXIII/actualites.php?a=80 |title=Carcassonne vs. Widnes |accessdate=2010-01-28 |publisher=com1site.com |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100401184902/http://www.com1site.com/FFRXIII/actualites.php?a=80 |archivedate=2010-04-01 |url-status=dead }} spectators. The Vikings had a pre-season friendly lined up against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] the following day, taking this into account Widnes sent a combination of players from the academy and the reserve side with players ranging from sixteen to twenty one years of age.{{cite web |url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=737 |title=Lost in France |accessdate=2010-01-27 |publisher=Widnes Vikings |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111001002432/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=737 |archivedate=2011-10-01 |url-status=dead }} This was the first time that the majority of the team had experienced open age rugby against a Carcassonne consisting of professionals. Two minutes in, Widnes lost full back Dave Kennedy, one of the more experienced players, through injury. Kennedy was replaced by Dave Dourley. After 8 minutes, a kick from Danny Craven was caught by Mark Briodie to score the opening try of the match, with Craven converting for the extra points. The Carcassonne forwards were not making much progress but had a good kicking game. Widnes gave away a few penalties early on but were otherwise faultless in defence. A 40–20 by Dave Dourley set up a scrum out on the right and from the scrum good work by Gregg Scott and Tom Rigby sent Chris Moogan over after 23 mins. The Vikings were penalised for a knock on shortly after the kick off and Carcassonne made the most of the opportunity and after good work from Andreas Bauer went over wide right on 27 mins. Substitutions were coming thick and fast and the French side's half backs came into fruition. A good handling move resulted in a try under the posts after 32 mins. Carcassonne converted making it 10 – 10 at the halftime interval. The first set of the second half resulted in a try for Adam Lawton but the conversion was missed by Craven. Carcassonne began to make the most of their possession and on 47 mins juggled the ball over the line to score out wide on the left but failed to make the conversion. The Vikings were penalised for another knock on, which gave Carcassonne the ball close to the Widnes line. Carcassonne again made the most of the opportunity and scored in the corner. This was followed up by a successful conversion to lead 20 - 14. Widnes' lack of match fitness was beginning to show, the Vikings made a few breaks but could not capitalise. Carcassonne kept the ball alive on their possession and their prop crashed over for another try and kicked the conversion to take the lead 26 - 14. Widnes had chances but were giving up possession too easily. The scoring was completed after 67 mins when another good passing move resulted in a Carcassonne try. The conversion was successful give Carcassonne a 32 - 14 lead which they held onto until the end of the match. ====Round 5: vs. Salford==== {{Rugbyleaguebox collapsible |round = Round 5 |date = 24 January 2010 |time = 15:00 GMT |team1 = {{league icon|Widnes Vikings|15}} Widnes Vikings |team2 = {{league icon|Salford City Reds|15}} Salford City Reds |score = 0–36 |stadium = [[Halton Stadium|Stobart Stadium Halton]] |location = [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire]] |attendance = |referee = [[James Child]] (Field) |points1 = |points2 = '''Tries:''' {{ubl|[[Karl Fitzpatrick]] x2|[[Steve Tyrer]]x2|[[Ashley Gibson]]||[[Adam Sidlow]]|[[Luke Adamson]]|[[Jodie Broughton]]}} '''Goals:''' {{ubl|[[Stefan Ratchford]] x2}} |competition = Trial |manofmatch = |report = |1sthalf = 0–16 |2ndhalf = 0–20 }} [[File:Widnes Vikings vs Salford City Reds 240110 001.JPG|thumb|left|''Widnes Vikings line up in their alternative strip for their home game against Salford City Reds, after Salford turned up in their white strip.''|alt= Widnes Vikings line up in their alternative strip for their home game against Salford City Reds, after Salford turned up in their white strip.]]Widnes suffered another setback in the pre-season preparations after losing 36–0 to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] at the [[Halton Stadium|Stobart Stadium Halton]].{{cite web |url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=712 |title=Salford City Reds too hot for Vikings |accessdate=2010-01-24 |publisher=Widnes Vikings |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128102204/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article.php?id=712 |archivedate=2010-01-28 |url-status=dead }} Salford fielded their strongest possible side for the friendly match in preparation for the upcoming [[Super League]] season. Amongst the Salford line-up were three former Vikings players [[Karl Fitzpatrick]], [[Steve Tyrer|Stephen Tyrer]] and [[Adam Sidlow]], who scored five tries between them. This is the second time in a week that the Vikings have faced a Super League side after they were defeated last Tuesday (19 January 2010) by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Widnes were missing seven senior first team players from the starting line-up, and the younger, inexperienced Vikings side could not cope with the pressure from the Super League side. Widnes had a few moments early on but a tight Salford defence was able to shut them out. The game lacked any real excitement and was very much a stop start affair, with referee [[James Child]] taking centre stage after interrupting the game 27 times for penalties. The referee was also the centre of a controversial decision when he dismissed [[Thomas Coyle (rugby league)|Tommy Coyle]] and [[Karl Fitzpatrick]] after Fitzpatrick struck Coyle after the hooker had delivered a loose high tackle which Widnes Head Coach Paul Cullen said wasn't a sending off offence.{{cite web|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby_league/s/1190200_fitzpatrick_sees_red_in_friendly |title=Fitzpatrick sees red in friendly |date=25 January 2010 |accessdate=2010-01-25 |publisher=Manchester Evening News |archiveurl=https://archive.today/20130421045108/http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby_league/s/1190200_fitzpatrick_sees_red_in_friendly |archivedate=2013-04-21 |url-status=live }} Fitzpatrick was then involved in a further incident when he threw his sports drink into a section of Widnes supporters before heading down the tunnel to the dressing room.{{cite web|url=http://reds.originalreddevils.com/2010/01/25/fitzpatrick-issues-apology-to-vikings/ |title=Fitzpatrick Issues Apology To Vikings |accessdate=2010-01-25 |publisher=Salford City Reds |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111006095448/http://reds.originalreddevils.com/2010/01/25/fitzpatrick-issues-apology-to-vikings/ |archivedate=2011-10-06 |url-status=live }} As a result of the red card Fitzpatrick could miss the start of the Super League season through suspension. {{Quote box | quote =Salford were very good and you have to take into account the opposition here and that they are in Super League | source = [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] | width =250px | align =right }} Salford put points on the board early on after Fitzpatrick went over the line after only two minutes of play and again a few moments later for his second. Salford continued the scoring with tries from Steve Tyrer and [[Ashley Gibson]].{{cite web|url=http://reds.originalreddevils.com/2010/01/24/reds-complete-pre-season-in-rampant-fashion/ |title=Reds Complete Pre-Season In Rampant Fashion |accessdate=2010-01-24 |publisher=Salford City Reds |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20111006095548/http://reds.originalreddevils.com/2010/01/24/reds-complete-pre-season-in-rampant-fashion/ |archivedate=2011-10-06 |url-status=live }} Salford took a sixteen-point lead into the break and added another twenty points in the second half with tries from Steve Tyrer, Adam Sidlow, [[Jodie Broughton]] and [[Luke Adamson]]. [[Stefan Ratchford]] added the extra points with two successful conversions. Vikings head coach Paul Cullen can take some comfort in knowing that this is a new squad and he won't be facing Super League opposition week in week out this season, he said: We won't be playing that calibre of opposition every week. Today was all about using this match to try to toughen ourselves up and if that's all we've got out of it, so be it.{{cite web|url=http://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/2010/01/25/old-boys-come-back-to-put-widnes-vikings-in-the-red-100252-25675324/ |title=Old boys come back to put Widnes Vikings in the Red |date=25 January 2010 |accessdate=2010-01-25 |publisher=Liverpool Echo |archiveurl=https://archive.today/20100126142013/http://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/2010/01/25/old-boys-come-back-to-put-widnes-vikings-in-the-red-100252-25675324/ |archivedate=2010-01-26 |url-status=live }} Salford were very good and you have to take into account the opposition here and that they are in Super League. We had a number of key players missing, but it's a trial game and the real work starts next week.{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-match-reports/2010/01/25/widnes-vikings-0-salford-city-reds-36-full-match-report-55368-25678441/ |title=Widnes Vikings 0 Salford City Reds 36 - full match report |accessdate=2010-01-25 |publisher=Runcorn and Widnes Weekly News |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727022634/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-match-reports/2010/01/25/widnes-vikings-0-salford-city-reds-36-full-match-report-55368-25678441/ |archivedate=2011-07-27 |url-status=live }} Karl Fitzpatrick issued a public apology the following day to the Widnes supports for his conduct as he left the field after his red card. Fitzpatrick offered to personally meet and apologise to any Vikings supporters that were affected by his actions. Director of Football for Salford Steve Simms said: "Karl was shook up by our version of events and he has no recollection of any of it. The blow to the head has knocked the sense out of him. However, none of this condones what happened out there, for which Karl and the Club are deeply apologetic". ==Regular season== ===Fixtures and results=== ====National League Cup==== {{See also|2010 Northern Rail Cup}} The Widnes Vikings are competing in [[National League Cup (rugby)|National League Cup]], known as the '''Northern Rail Cup''' for sponsorship reasons. Cup}} The 2010 Challenge Cup (officially known as the 2010 '''Carnegie Challenge Cup''' for sponsorship reasons) is a [[Single-elimination tournament|knockout]] rugby league tournament which began its preliminary stages in January. The Challenge Cup is the most competitive European rugby league tournament at club level and is open to teams from [[England]], [[Wales]], [[Scotland]], [[France]] and [[Russia]]. The final of the Challenge Cup is one of the most prestigious matches in world rugby league, and is traditionally held at [[Wembley Stadium]], [[London]]. Despite London not being an area traditionally associated with rugby league, the final receives a lot of mainstream media coverage and is broadcast to many different countries around the world. Super League side [[Warrington Wolves]] are the reigning champions, following their 16–25 victory over [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the 2009 Challenge Cup Final at Wembley Stadium on 29 August 2009. The Co-Operative Championship is a semi-professional rugby league football competition played in the United Kingdom and France, one tier below the first tier [[Super League]]. The two worst performing teams during the season, with the exception of [[Toulouse Olympique]], will be relegated to Championship 1. There is no automatic promotion from this league to Super League, which uses a licensing system which renewed every three years. Qualifying for the grand final is a prerequisite for Championship clubs to be able to apply for license in the next round of applications for the [[2012–14 Super League licences|2012–14 period]]. The 2010 Co-Operative Championship season will consist of two stages. The regular season is played over 22 [[Round-robin tournament|round-robin]] fixtures, in which each of the eleven teams involved in the competition will play each other once at [[Home (sports)|home]] and once [[Road (sports)|away]]. In the 2010 Co-Operative Championship, a win is worth three points in the table, a draw is worth one two points apiece, and a loss by 12 points or fewer is worth 1 bonus point. The league leaders at the end of the regular season will receive the league leaders trophy, but the Championship is decided through the second stage of the season via a [[playoffs]] system. The top six teams in the table will contest to play in the grand final, the winners of which are crowned 2010 Co-Operative Champions. {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" width="100%" !
